query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Canter', 'To move at such pace', 'Critics argued that the plan might canter the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Canto', 'One of the chief divisions of a long poem, a book', 'The essay highlights the canto of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cantonment', 'Temporary military living quarters', 'The passage presents a cantonment portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Canva', 'To cover an area or object', 'Critics argued that the plan might canva the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Capacious', 'Having a lot of space; roomy', '“The capacious backpack easily fit all of his belongings.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Capillary', 'Of or pertaining to hair', 'The author hoped the new policy would capillary tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Capitulate', 'To surrender or give up resistance', 'The author hoped the new policy would capitulate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Caprice', 'A sudden and unaccountable change of mood or behavior', 'Her caprice became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Capricious', 'Given to sudden changes in mood or behavior', '“His capricious nature made it difficult to predict his decisions.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Captious', 'Apt to notice and make much of trivial defaults', 'Over time, the community learned to captious its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Captivate', 'Attract and hold interest and attention of, charm', 'Over time, the community learned to captivate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Carcass', 'Body of a dead animal', 'Her carcass became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cardiac', 'Pertaining to the heart', 'The author hoped the new policy would cardiac tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cardinal', 'Of fundamental importance, crucial, pivotal', 'Scholars noted a striking cardinal between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Careen', 'Turn (a ship) of its side for cleaning or repair', 'The essay highlights the careen of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Caret', 'A mark made in writing to show something is to be inserted', 'The author hoped the new policy would caret tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Caricature', 'To represent someone in an exaggerated or distorted manner', 'Over time, the community learned to caricature its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Carnage', 'Death and destruction', 'The essay highlights the carnage of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Carnal', 'Relating to the physical and especially sexual appetites', 'Over time, the community learned to carnal its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Carnivorous', 'Feeding on other animals, meat-eaters', 'The passage presents a carnivorous portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Carouse', 'To engage in a noisy or drunken social gathering', 'The author hoped the new policy would carouse tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Carp', 'To complain about a fault, to harp on', 'The author hoped the new policy would carp tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Carrion', 'Dead flesh, carcasses', 'Scholars noted a striking carrion between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cartilage', 'A type of dense, non-vascular connective tissue', 'Her cartilage became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cartographer', 'The study and practice of making maps', 'Her cartographer became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cartridge', 'A container holding a quantity of ink or other substance', 'The essay highlights the cartridge of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Caste', 'Any hereditary social class of south asian societies', 'The essay highlights the caste of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Castigate', 'To reprimand severely', 'The author hoped the new policy would castigate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Casualty', 'Something that happens by chance', 'Her casualty became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Casuistry', 'Oversubtle reasoning in questions of morality', 'Her casuistry became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cat-o-nine-tail', 'A whip, usually having nine knotted cords, used for flogging', 'Scholars noted a striking cat-o-nine-tail between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cataclysm', 'A sudden, violent event', 'Her cataclysm became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Catalogue', 'A list or record, systematically arranged', 'Scholars noted a striking catalogue between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Catalyst', 'Something that speeds up a reaction or change', '“The invention of the internet was a catalyst for global communication.”', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Catapult', 'A device that launches an object some distance', 'The essay highlights the catapult of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cataract', 'A waterspout', 'Scholars noted a striking cataract between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Catastrophe', 'A disaster', 'Scholars noted a striking catastrophe between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Categorical', 'Unambiguously explicit and direct', 'What seemed categorical at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Caterwaul', 'To have a noisy argument', 'Over time, the community learned to caterwaul its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Catharsis', 'The process of releasing strong emotions', 'The essay highlights the catharsis of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cathode', 'The electrode by which current leaves an electrolytic cell', 'The essay highlights the cathode of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Catholic', 'All embracing, general', 'The essay highlights the catholic of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Catholicism', 'The faith, practice and order of the roman catholic church', 'Her catholicism became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Caucus', 'To hold or form such a group or meeting', 'Critics argued that the plan might caucus the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Causal', 'Of, relating to, or being a cause of something, causing', 'The essay highlights the causal of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Caustic', 'Sarcastic and bitter in tone', 'The passage presents a caustic portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cauterize', 'To burn, sear, or freeze tissue for curative purposes', 'Critics argued that the plan might cauterize the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cavalcade', 'A company of riders', 'Scholars noted a striking cavalcade between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Caveat', 'To qualify a particular statement with a proviso or caveat', 'Over time, the community learned to caveat its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cavil', 'To make petty or unnecessary objections', 'Critics argued that the plan might cavil the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
